There's somebody out there cooking burgers and hot dogs for everybody. There's the ride. There's the go watch the cows get milked. There's the fake cow milk thing the kids do with the fake cow. And there's a competition. And there's all these activities and stuff. And the year I was there I'm pretty sure over a two day period of time over 4,000 people. Roughly 50% customer, 50% non-customer come to this thing and they're there for three and four and five hours. And they're meeting and greeting staff and they're sampling product. They directly sign people up right there to become customers. But even when they don't. And this kind of event there's a chiropractor for many years in Florida who used to come to my seminars who had a big property with a lake and everything. And he ran his two day customer appreciation event very similar to this except no cows. But it was bring everybody you want. And there's boats to go out on the lake on. There's cookout. There's a movie on big screen at night. And he would have eight, nine thousand people go through this place. And patients would bring six, seven, ten, twelve people. They would make the people had their class reunions during his thing at his place. And he said it's all I gotta do. It's it. I do that and I'm done for the year. I can't take any more patients. There was an insurance agent in Phoenix for years. He had his birthday thing. He had a birthday thing and he had a St. Patrick's Day thing. In both times he just rented out this bar put up big tents. At his birthday he sat up on a big throne the whole time and invited customers but they were supposed to bring everybody you want and you had to bring a gag gift. And you could donate to charity if you wanted to. But it was free booze, free food. And this thing grew from a few hundred to a thousand to a thousand. So he told me the same thing. He said I really don't have to do any other marketing. He said this pretty much gives me all the client referrals I could ever possibly want and I'm busy. In B2B sort of summer's white same thing with an annual dinner before the National Speaker's Association convention. So there's the you know implant seminar. There's the cosmetic surgery seminar. There's the how not to have back pain class that you encourage customers, clients, patients to bring people to. If you're really aggressive we always talk to chiropractors. One of the parts of becoming a new patient was mandatory attendance at the back-care class within two weeks of being a patient and you had to bring a buddy. Oh wow. Wow. And if you didn't you got fired as a patient. Wow. So we forced it. So there's that category of activity. Then there's this other category of activity that is more about just letting people be introduced to you. In a fun. In a fun and you know not salesy. Not you know. Yeah because that's how you ruin it. Right. With one of those events right. I created for the financial advisors. I created to do it right around Father's Day baseball night in the office if you got a big enough office. Put a big screen TV in. Get some DVDs of old baseball movies. And pick clips and get some hot dogs and popcorn and beer. And no pitch. No stand up. No do nothing. Just all the clients. Bring your kids and bring your buddies to baseball night. And I got my literature over here. I got copies of my books over here. If anybody wants them fine. If you got a money question and you want to come ask me feel free and let's have a good time. Right. Right. And people will bring people to that and people will come to that who if you say hey the next time I'm doing my seminar. Come and bring your buddy. Yeah. Right. So you think how much higher that hurdle is than come with me. My financial advisor is doing baseball night. Oh great. Yeah. Exactly. There's actual clips from the event.
